sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 103

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 103:1
items.html:
(to the question) A third of the measure of the Yesod
In every Sefira there are three thirds, which are the discernments of GE and GAR of Bina, as is known, for only these remained above Tzimtzum Bet [the second restriction], while the AHP of every degree fell below it. And they are the main essence of the degree, for the AHP that are returned at the time of Gadlut are not counted as principal in the Partzuf, but only as discernments of addition — except for the Kli of the Yesod, which could not receive from the GAR of Bina, because Bina "spreads only as far as Hod," as is known. Therefore the lower third of the secret of the Malchut was joined to it, and became its own Atara [crown]. And this is the first sweetening of the Malchut, in that it became a part of the essence of the Yesod, namely its lower third. (p. 762, Item 46; and p. 763, beginning "Therefore")
